title: Complete DID method resolution algorithm per W3C DID Core §7

artifacts.description: Extend existing DID method spec with §7-conformant resolution algorithm: resolve(), dereference(), and error code table (invalidDid, notFound, deactivated, methodNotSupported, internalError)

TypeScript implementation of resolve(did, resolutionOptions) returning {didDocument, didResolutionMetadata, didDocumentMetadata}. Parses did:bolyra:<commitment>, queries IdentityRegistry, builds DID Document with BabyJubjub verificationMethod (JsonWebKey2020, crv=Baby-Jubjub), sets deactivated flag for revoked identities
Constructs the DID Document object: id, @context, verificationMethod array, authentication, assertionMethod. Encodes BabyJubjub public key as JWK with crv=Baby-Jubjub
Add getIdentityStatus(bytes32 commitment) view returning (bool enrolled, bool revoked, uint256 enrolledAt, bytes32 babyJubjubPubKeyX, bytes32 babyJubjubPubKeyY) to support on-chain resolution queries

Unit tests covering: valid DID resolves to correct document, revoked DID sets deactivated=true, invalid DID syntax returns invalidDid error, unknown DID returns notFound error, verificationMethod encodes correct JWK
W3C DID Test Suite conformance runner: validates resolve() return shape matches DID Core §7.1 exactly, checks all required metadata fields, runs against W3C test vectors
Developer guide: how to instantiate the resolver, configure RPC endpoint for IdentityRegistry, handle resolution errors, integrate with did-resolver universal resolver interface

steps: Audit spec/did-method-bolyra.md to inventory what DID syntax and commitment derivation are already defined; identify gaps against W3C DID Core §7.1 resolution algorithm requirements
Add getIdentityStatus() view function to IdentityRegistry.sol returning enrollment bool, revocation bool, enrolledAt timestamp, and packed BabyJubjub pubkey coordinates (X, Y as uint256)
Implement DID parser in sdk/src/resolver.ts: validate did:bolyra:<commitment> syntax, extract commitment string, return invalidDid metadata on parse failure

Implement did-document-builder.ts: construct @context array ([https://www.w3.org/ns/did/v1, crv extension context]), build verificationMethod entry with type=JsonWebKey2020 and publicKeyJwk.crv=Baby-Jubjub using X/Y coordinates from registry

Implement resolve(did, resolutionOptions) core: call IdentityRegistry.getIdentityStatus(), handle not-enrolled (notFound), build DID Document via builder, set didDocumentMetadata.deactivated=true if revoked, populate didResolutionMetadata.contentType=application/did+ld+json
Implement resolutionOptions.accept content negotiation: support application/did+ld+json and application/did+json; return representationNotSupported error for unknown types
Wire resolver to an ethers.js provider so RPC endpoint is configurable at instantiation; expose BolyraResolver class with constructor({ rpcUrl, registryAddress })
Write unit tests in sdk/test/resolver.test.ts using Hardhat in-process network: mock IdentityRegistry, test all error branches and the happy path
Write W3C conformance tests in spec/conformance/did-resolution-conformance.test.ts validating exact shape of return triple against DID Core §7.1 normative requirements
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md with Resolution section: algorithm pseudocode, error code table, verificationMethod encoding spec for BabyJubjub JWK, and deactivation semantics
Export resolver from sdk/src/index.ts as part of public API; update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with resolver usage example
Run full test suite (npm test) and confirm all resolver tests pass; verify no regressions in circuit or contract tests
dependencies: IdentityRegistry.sol deployed with getIdentityStatus() view function
BabyJubjub public key stored in IdentityRegistry at enrollment time
JsonWebKey2020 context and Baby-Jubjub JWK crv value registration
did-resolver npm package interface (for universal resolver compatibility)
Hardhat local network for integration tests
